cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user is asking about a proverb "Time is money" and the question is what it means. The options are A to D. The original text says the user thinks time is more valuable than money because once money is spent, you can earn it back, but time never returns. The question is asking what the phrase "Time is money" means according to the options given. Let me look at the options: A) Time is equal to dollars, pounds... (This is taking it literally, as in time being currency) B) Time is gold. (This is another common proverb, but the question is about "Time is money", not "Time is gold") C) Time is valuable. (This is the underlying meaning of the proverb, emphasizing value) D) We must spend time in buying goods. (This seems to take a literal approach, but that's not the meaning) The passage the user wrote expands on the idea that time is more valuable than money because it cannot be recovered.
